arts.ttl: Serie A | Roma v Bologna: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: As Roma prepares to host Bologna in a pivotal Serie A encounter, both managers are grappling with lineup decisions and injury concerns. Roma’s coach Ivan Juric is expected to make several alterations to his starting eleven following their midweek Europa League match in Belgium. Top scorer Artem Dovbyk is likely to return to the lineup, providing a boost to Roma’s attack. However, the team will have to cope without the creative talents of Paulo Dybala, who has been ruled out due to injury. Dybala surprised by exclusion from Roma-Bologna match, seeks explanation from club
arts.sum: Paulo Dybala has reportedly been caught off guard by his exclusion from AS Roma’s upcoming match against Bologna. Gazzetta dello Sport report that the Argentine forward was apparently unaware of his omission until coach Ivan Juric publicly announced it during a pre-match press conference. According to sources close to the player, Dybala believed he was fit and ready to participate not only in the Bologna game but also in the midweek European fixture in Belgium. The player and his team were expecting to conduct a fitness test during today’s training session, following a negative MRI scan performed yesterday. arts.ttl: Roma vs Bologna, a game with European implications
arts.sum: Bologna have won their last two Serie A matches against Roma, and could win three successive games against the Capitolini for the first time in the competition since the period between 1958 and 1961 (five in that case, under Alfredo Foni first and Federico Allasio then). Bologna have won their last two league matches without conceding; the Emiliani could collect three successive wins while keeping a clean sheet in each of those for the first time in Serie A since 1973 (one of the three meetings against Roma, won 1-0 at Olimpico).

arts.ttl: Mystery surrounds Dybala’s availability as Juric’s announcement contradicts planned fitness test
arts.sum: Roma manager Ivan Juric has announced that Paulo Dybala will be unavailable for the upcoming Serie A match against Bologna at the Olimpico. During his pre-match press conference, Juric stated, “Dybala felt discomfort during warm-up, and although he wanted to train yesterday, the discomfort persists, so he won’t be available.” This statement seemed to provide a clear picture of the situation, suggesting a cautious approach to protect the talented Argentine player, who has been prone to physical issues throughout his career, especially in recent years. However, Sky Sports has revealed an intriguing twist to the story.
